findings.evidence: `Prod.lean:49` has `@[expose] public section`; `Prod.lean:219-225` defines `twistedProd` and already provides the `[simp]` pointwise characterization.
findings.fix: Add `@[no_expose]` to `twistedProd` and keep `twistedProd_apply` as the characteristic lemma; add any further explicit lemmas consumers need instead of relying on unfolding.
findings.issue: `twistedProd` is introduced inside the enclosing `@[expose] public section`, so its body `ω₁.prod (ω₂.rescale (-1) ...)` becomes part of the public reduction API even though line 218 says downstream code should use `twistedProd_apply`.

findings.evidence: `SymplecticTransport.lean:118-121` hides `IsSymplectomorphism`, and `SymplecticTransport.lean:148-151` proves the identity case without `@[simp]`; nearby precedent is `AlmostComplex.lean:198` where `isComplexLinearMap_id` is `@[simp]`.
findings.fix: Mark `IsSymplectomorphism.refl` with `@[simp]`, matching the existing identity-map convention for similar predicates.
findings.issue: The normal form that the identity equivalence is a symplectomorphism is not tagged for automation, so `simp` cannot close the basic identity case of the new hidden predicate.

summary: The new symplectomorphism/graph API is mostly characterized, but the public surface still exposes an implementation body that the PR itself says consumers should not unfold, and the identity normal form is missing from simp.
